sourceMovie.title: Collateral
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Collateral (2004) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Drive
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Collateral clicked for you, Drive (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Usual Suspects
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Collateral clicked for you, The Usual Suspects (1995) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Collateral?
faq.answer: Try Drive, The Usual Suspects, Jackie Brown—each shares DNA with Collateral while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Collateral?
faq.answer: Collateral (2004) is best described as Drama, Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Collateral worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
